metadata.recent_signal: Launched ElevateOS on April 21 2026 -- first agentic OS for revenue enablement built on MCP and A2A standards. Actively hiring SDE-III and SDE-II for CoE-ML team.
metadata.match_reason: Public JD lists GenAI/LLM, agentic workflows, prompt engineering, RAG, AWS/K8s/CI-CD; internal LLM Gateway; CoE-ML team building Copilot, AI Role Plays, ElevateOS.
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: ElevateOS just launched as first agentic OS for revenue enablement. CoE-ML is hiring SDE-III for GenAI/LLM/agentic work. Amit's production RAG (50K/month, BGE-M3 pipeline, multi-tenant RBAC) and multi-model agentic orchestration are a direct match.
metadata.notes: Pune HQ right for Amit. 501-1K employees. Email pattern: first.last@mindtickle.com (93% per LeadIQ). ElevateOS built on MCP+A2A. Uses Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Q internally.

research_summary: Mindtickle's CoE-ML team is hiring SDE-III for GenAI/LLM/agentic workflows (LangGraph, CrewAI, LiteLLM, pgvector, AWS/K8s). The company just launched ElevateOS (April 21 2026) -- first agentic OS for revenue enablement built on MCP and A2A. Hitesh Nankani (SDE-III, AI team lead) attended the first Claude Code meetup in India on May 13 and posted about production LLMOps being the real gap beyond prompt engineering. Ajay Dubey (Sr. Engineering Manager, GenAI infra) posted the SDE-III COE-ML referral. Email pattern is first.last@mindtickle.com. 6 contacts captured: 2 Tier 1, 1 Tier 3, 3 Tier 4.
jd_full_text:
Mindtickle -- Software Development Engineer III (AI/ML - GenAI/LLM)
Location: Pune, India | Bengaluru, India

About Mindtickle:
Mindtickle is the leading AI-powered revenue enablement platform that combines on-the-job learning and deal execution to drive behavior change and get more revenue per rep. The CoE-ML (Center of Excellence for Machine Learning) team is at the heart of Mindtickle's mission -- embedding AI across the product in the form of CoPilots, hyper-realistic AI-powered role plays, automated content curation, and intelligent coaching. Engineers work with cutting-edge GenAI and LLM technologies while maintaining production systems at scale.

About the Role:
As an SDE-3 in CoE-ML, you will translate business asks and requirements into technical requirements, solutions, architectures, and implementations.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead the end-to-end design, prototyping, and implementation of AI systems ensuring they meet performance, scalability, and reliability targets
- Architect solutions for GenAI and LLM integrations, including prompt engineering, context management, and agentic workflows
- Develop and maintain production-grade code with high test coverage and robust CI/CD pipelines on AWS, Kubernetes, and cloud-native infrastructures
- Establish and maintain post-deployment monitoring, performance testing, and alerting frameworks to ensure performance and quality SLAs are met
- Conduct thorough design and code reviews, uphold best practices, drive technical excellence across the team
- Mentor SDE-1/SDE-2 engineers, provide technical guidance and career development support
- Lead design discussions, pair-programming sessions, and brown-bag talks on emerging AI/ML topics
- Work cross-functionally with product, QA, data engineering, and DevOps
- Design and deploy production-grade AI/ML components including LLM-powered features, RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and model inference services
- Build and maintain evaluation frameworks using Maxim, LangFuse, or Weights & Biases
- Define and track quality metrics including precision, recall, BLEU, ROUGE, and LLM-as-judge scores
- Monitor AI services using Datadog, Prometheus, Grafana
- Use AI gateway platforms like LiteLLM, Portkey, TrueFoundry for cost tracking and governance
- Instrument agentic workflows built on LangGraph or CrewAI
- Use AI-assisted development tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) as standard practice

Required Skills:
- 5+ years of professional software development experience with 2+ years in production AI/ML systems
- Python (primary), Java or Go
- Experience building or operating ML pipelines, LLM-based features, or data-intensive services
- AWS and containerized deployments (Docker, Kubernetes)
- Agentic frameworks: LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI or similar
- LLM observability and evaluation platforms (Maxim, LangFuse, Weights & Biases)
- Machine learning fundamentals including model training, evaluation, and deployment tradeoffs

Preferred:
- Vector databases and embedding-based retrieval (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector)
- Hands-on prompt engineering with chain-of-thought, few-shot, structured output patterns
- AI gateway platforms for LLM cost governance
- Open-source LLMs (Hugging Face) and fine-tuning workflows
- AI coding assistants in daily development workflows
tailoring_notes: Summary: rewritten to lead with LangGraph/LangChain and LLMOps, mirroring JD's 'agentic workflows' and 'production LLM systems' language. Skills: reordered to put LLMs-and-Techniques first (JD's primary signal), Vector DBs second, Infrastructure third (added Kubernetes for their K8s requirement), Languages fourth; dropped Hardware/IoT category (irrelevant for CoE-ML role). Projects: replaced Voicer (TinyML) + WeepScope (audio CNN) with P1 Multi-Tenant RAG Chatbot and P2 AI Video Course Generation Pipeline -- JD primary signal is agentic AI / LLM orchestration / multi-model, exact match to P1+P2 per selection guide.

contacts.contact_role: Senior Engineering Manager -- GenAI & Agentic AI Infrastructure
contacts.research_hook: He posted the SDE-III COE-ML referral in March/April 2026, describing the team as reshaping sales enablement with LLMs, NLP, and speech AI. Leads GenAI & Agentic AI infra at Mindtickle -- LLMOps, prompt engineering, AI observability. Previously at AppLift and Groupon, IIIT-H.
contacts.subject: question about CoE-ML's LLMOps setup
contacts.content:
Hi Ajay,

Came across your post about the SDE-III COE-ML opening a few weeks ago. Your focus on production LLMOps and AI observability as the actual hard problem is pretty much what I've been working on for the last two years.

At Silver Touch, rebuilt a production RAG system serving 50K+ queries/month: moved from FAISS to Milvus, benchmarked 6 embedding models, got MRR from 45% to 95%. At Vivansh now, the same system went agentic with LangGraph and got a full 2-stage RBAC retrieval layer (250+ permissions per query, Milvus permission index cross-referenced with PostgreSQL). The evaluation pipeline is custom too, because off-the-shelf eval frameworks don't handle multi-tenant access control well.

If the CoE-ML team is still hiring on the SDE-III front, I'd appreciate 15 minutes. Tuesday or Thursday works on my end.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: question about CoE-ML's LLMOps setup
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Ajay,

Bumping this up in case it got buried. Since sending the last email, I finished a short benchmark comparing LiteLLM and Portkey for cost governance on a multi-model setup. Interesting tradeoffs on the latency side. Happy to share if it's relevant to what the CoE-ML infrastructure looks like.

If now's not a good time, no worries.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: question about CoE-ML's LLMOps setup
contacts.follow_up_2_content:

Last note from me on this. If the team isn't actively hiring right now, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar for when it opens up.

Either way, good luck with ElevateOS.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: SDE-III (AI & Engineering) -- leads AI initiatives and org-wide AI adoption
contacts.research_hook: May 13 2026 (4 days ago): attended the first Claude Code meetup in India and posted about the production LLMOps gap -- 'shipping a feature is easy, building the infrastructure that makes AI predictable and scalable is where the real work is.' 3 weeks ago: published 'One Loop, Three Patterns: A Practical Guide to Multi-Agent Orchestration' in Towards AI. January 2026: led meta-prompting workshop at Mindtickle, built in-house tools supporting 7+ projects in 60 days.
contacts.subject: the Claude Code meetup post about Day 2 LLMOps
contacts.content:
Hi Hitesh,

Read your post from the Claude Code meetup on May 13. The part that landed: most people treating the prompt as the entire solution, while the actual work is E2E LLMOps, automated evaluation, and the orchestration that makes a model reliable in production. Cleaner diagnosis than I've seen from most engineers in this space.

For the last year I've been in exactly that space: a production RAG system at 50K+ queries/month where the hard part wasn't the retrieval, it was the eval pipeline. LangFuse didn't handle multi-tenant RBAC edge cases well, so I built a custom evaluation layer. Benchmarked BGE-M3 against 5 other embedding models on production data, built a 2-stage Milvus + PostgreSQL permission index for 250+ access control checks per query, got MRR from 45% to 95%. Now at Vivansh, the system runs as a LangGraph agentic workflow.

Applying for the SDE-III COE-ML role. If you're open to a short call, I'd like to hear how ElevateOS's orchestration layer handles the Day 2 problems in practice.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: the Claude Code meetup post about Day 2 LLMOps
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Hitesh,

Following up in case this slipped by. Since reaching out, I read your 'One Loop, Three Patterns' piece in Towards AI. The distinction between orchestration patterns is something I ran into firsthand: the AI Course Builder I shipped at Vivansh uses a different loop structure than the permission-aware RAG system, but both need the same eval discipline.

If there's a fit for the SDE-III role, happy to go deeper on how either system is architected.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: the Claude Code meetup post about Day 2 LLMOps
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Hitesh,

Last note from me. If timing doesn't work or the team is full, no worries. Would appreciate being on your radar for future openings on the CoE-ML side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.contact_role: SDE-II (Agentic AI) -- built Seller Copilot using RAG
contacts.research_hook: Built Mindtickle's agentic Seller Copilot using RAG to help sellers close deals faster; reduced unit testing time 50% using GenAI tools. LinkedIn headline includes 'Agentic AI' explicitly. DA-IICT grad working in Pune on Mindtickle's AI product layer.
contacts.subject: RAG and RBAC for seller copilots
contacts.content:
Hi Aayush,

Noticed you built Mindtickle's Seller Copilot using RAG. That's a tough problem to get right, especially when the retrieval has to stay permission-aware across different sellers and deal contexts.

I spent the last year doing similar work: multi-tenant RAG system at 50K+ queries/month with a 2-stage retrieval pipeline enforcing 250+ RBAC permissions per query, Milvus for vector search and PostgreSQL for access control cross-referencing. MRR went from 45% to 95% after swapping the embedding stack and tuning retrieval. At Vivansh now, the system runs as a LangGraph agentic workflow.

Applying for the SDE-III COE-ML role. If you think there's a fit, happy if you can pass this along to whoever's running the hiring.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

Amit
